title: Bridge Essay: Neuronal dynamics of slow and fast-motion motor imagery.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Neuronal, dynamics, of, slow, and.

**Neuronal dynamics of slow and fast-motion motor imagery.** — Neuroscience. PMID: 40482699. DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroscience.2025.06.002. Motor imagery (MI) is acognitive process requiring mental simulation of physical actions, engaging neural networks that overlap with those activated during actual execution. This study investigated the neural correlates of slow and fast MI in ten healthy adolescent athletes instructed to mentally simulate asoccer dribbling task at low and high speeds. Brain activity was recorded using electroencephalography, focusing on alpha (8-12 Hz) and beta (18-28 Hz) wave frequencies. Results revealed dis
